deep_dive.title: TrioPose: Native Triple-Stream Diffusion Transformers for Pose-Guided Text-to-Image Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Pose-guided text-to-image generation often suffers from limb distortions and feature crosstalk in complex multi-person scenarios. While existing UNet-based adapters struggle with long-range spatial dependencies, emerging Multimodal Diffusion Transformers (MM-DiTs) offer superior global modeling. However, naive signal concatenation in MM-DiTs severely disrupts pre-trained latent distributions. To address this, we propose TrioPose, a native pose-driven framework built upon the SD3.5M architecture. Specifically, we introduce a Triple-Stream Pose-Aware DiT (TSPA-DiT) that treats pose as an independent modality. It employs layer-wise activation and zero-initialized dual-residual injection to smoothly enforce geometric constraints while preserving pre-trained latent stability. To resolve severe multi-instance occlusions, we design a Learnable Relational Bias Mask that categorizes topological connectivity into fine-grained physical states, mapping them into continuous attention soft constraints to effectively decouple inter-instance interference. Furthermore, a Pose-Guided Spatial Loss Weighting strategy modulates the native diffusion objective using heatmap-derived error maps, focusing anatomical supervision strictly on distortion-prone regions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that TrioPose achieves state-of-the-art performance across challenging benchmarks, including Human-Art, CrowdPose, and OCHuman. Notably, it attains an AP of $64.33$ on Human-Art, representing a $30\%$ improvement over prior arts, while setting new standards for visual fidelity and text-image semantic alignment in complex multi-human generation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multimodal diffusion transformer for pose-guided text-to-image generation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于姿态引导文本到图像生成的多模态扩散Transformer

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: TrioPose proposes a triple-stream pose-aware diffusion transformer that treats pose as an independent modality, enabling high-quality pose-guided text-to-image generation without disrupting pretrained latent distributions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有姿态引导文本到图像生成方法在多人物场景中存在肢体扭曲和特征串扰问题，多模态扩散Transformer虽能全局建模但直接信号拼接会干扰预训练潜在分布。TrioPose提出三重流姿态感知扩散Transformer（TSPA-DiT），将姿态作为独立模态，通过逐层激活和零初始化的双重残差注入平滑施加几何约束。实验表明，TrioPose在复杂多人物场景中生成质量显著优于现有方法。该方法为多模态扩散模型的结构设计提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: ShallowBench: Benchmarking Generative Drug Design Models on Shallow-Pocket Targets
deep_dive.abstract: While generative AI models have demonstrated remarkable success in structure-based drug design, they predominantly rely on deep binding pockets and struggle to sample effective ligands for challenging low-pocketability targets, such as the historically "undruggable" oncology targets KRAS and MYC. To address this gap, we introduce ShallowBench, a strictly curated benchmark of 5,780 shallow-pocket targets extracted from CrossDocked2020. By computing the difference between an Alpha Shape "lid" volume and the underlying protein atom voxel volume, we successfully isolated targets with low concavity while ensuring sufficient surface area for binding. Evaluating various state-of-the-art generative models reveals weaker predicted binding affinity on these low-concavity interfaces. ShallowBench therefore provides a rigorous benchmark for generative biology models and highlights the necessity of new architectural innovations or loss functions capable of navigating these challenging targets.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for generative drug design on shallow-pocket targets

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces ShallowBench, a benchmark of 5,780 shallow-pocket targets, revealing that current generative models struggle on low-pocketability proteins like KRAS.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对现有生成药物设计模型在低口袋性靶标上表现不佳的问题，构建了ShallowBench基准，包含5780个浅口袋靶标。通过Alpha Shape计算方法隔离出低凹度但表面积足够的靶标，系统评估了多种最先进的生成模型。结果表明现有模型在预测浅口袋靶标的结合亲和力上较弱，揭示了该领域的关键挑战，为未来方法改进提供了标准化测试平台。

deep_dive.title: Generative Molecular Morphing for Flexible-Size Design via Unbalanced Optimal Transport
deep_dive.abstract: The success of generative molecular design hinges on a model's steerability toward high-reward samples. Because many molecular properties are intrinsically linked to molecular size, accurately capturing the joint distribution of properties and the number of atoms is essential. However, current diffusion and flow-based models fix the number of atoms, which ultimately limits their ability to navigate this complex relationship. To address this, we introduce Morph, a flexible-size generative model for conditional and unconditional 3D molecular design based on geometric graphs. By dynamically adapting size, Morph can seamlessly integrate existing structural priors, like scaffolds, and significantly enhances property steering. We show that Morph matches current fixed-size state-of-the-art models while offering the benefit of unparalleled sampling flexibility. We demonstrate out-of-distribution generation in regimes where previous models fail, paving the way for enhanced generative modeling for molecular design.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: generative molecular design with flexible size, using unbalanced optimal transport
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 柔性尺寸分子生成，使用非平衡最优传输
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 柔性尺寸分子生成，使用非平衡最优传输
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 柔性尺寸分子生成，使用非平衡最优传输
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Morph introduces a flexible-size generative model for 3D molecular design via unbalanced optimal transport, enabling property steering beyond fixed-atom diffusion models.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有扩散和流模型固定原子数，限制了分子性质调控与尺寸的联合学习。本文提出Morph模型，基于几何图和非平衡最优传输动态调整分子大小，无缝整合结构先验如骨架。实验证明Morph在条件和无条件分子设计中显著增强了性质导向能力，并保持了化学有效性，为药物设计和材料科学提供了灵活的新工具。

quick_skim.title: AgentPLM: Agentic Protein Language Models with Reasoning-Augmented Decoding for Protein Sequence Design
quick_skim.abstract: Protein language models (PLMs) are passive oracles: they generate sequences in a single forward pass with no mechanism to consult external biophysical feedback or redirect generation when a candidate violates thermodynamic or structural constraints. We introduce AgentPLM, which addresses this by equipping a pre-trained PLM with i) Reasoning-Augmented Decoding (RAD), which interleaves autoregressive generation with tool calls (ESMFold, FoldX, AutoDock Vina), and ii) Contrastive Agent Policy Optimisation (CAPO), a trajectory-level extension of direct preference optimisation that trains the policy end-to-end to learn when oracle feedback is informative rather than merely imitating high-fitness sequences. We evaluate AgentPLM on benchmark tasks spanning de novo enzyme design, antibody optimisation, thermostability, PPI interface design, and zero-shot fitness prediction with standardised oracle APIs and controlled sequence-identity splits. AgentPLM achieves state-of-the-art results with a gain in antibody top-10% hit rate over the strongest passive baseline, providing mechanistic evidence of online error correction without explicit backtracking.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: protein sequence generation using deep learning with RL and tool calls
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用深度学习和强化学习进行蛋白质序列生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用深度学习和强化学习进行蛋白质序列生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用深度学习和强化学习进行蛋白质序列生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AgentPLM equips a protein language model with reasoning-augmented decoding and contrastive agent policy optimization to generate sequences that satisfy biophysical constraints, evaluated with tools like AutoDock Vina.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有蛋白质语言模型是静态的，无法在生成过程中利用外部生物物理反馈。AgentPLM通过集成推理增强解码和对比智能体策略优化，使预训练模型能够动态调用工具（如AutoDock Vina）来评估和改进候选序列。实验表明，该方法在生成满足热力学和结构约束的蛋白质序列方面优于基线。这项工作为结合深度学习与物理模拟的蛋白质设计提供了新范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有蛋白质语言模型是静态的，无法在生成过程中利用外部生物物理反馈。AgentPLM通过集成推理增强解码和对比智能体策略优化，使预训练模型能够动态调用工具（如AutoDock Vina）来评估和改进候选序列。实验表明，该方法在生成满足热力学和结构约束的蛋白质序列方面优于基线。这项工作为结合深度学习与物理模拟的蛋白质设计提供了新范式。

quick_skim.title: Structured drift design for denoising diffusion models
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion-based generative models have achieved remarkable success in high-dimensional data generation; however, they fundamentally rely on isotropic diffusion processes that destroy meaningful geometric structures in the forward process. For complex, multimodal, and highly correlated distributions such as biologically constrained genetic data, isotropic noise merges distinct modes and distorts intrinsic dependencies. This forces the reverse process to recover structure from heavily degraded signals, leading to slow convergence, mode averaging, and biologically implausible samples. To address this, we introduce the Geometry-aware Ornstein-Uhlenbeck (GOU) process, a structured drift design that embeds data geometry into forward and backward dynamics. By employing a variance-aware anisotropic drift, GOU contracts low-variance directions rapidly while preserving high-variance directions longer, maintaining key multimodal structures as stable channels over time. Crucially, we show that GOU's backward initialization error is governed by local rather than global variance. This geometry-adaptive initialization improves convergence rates by reducing initial mismatch and preserving cluster-level structures. Synthetic and real-world genetic experiments demonstrate that GOU significantly improves mode separation, correlation preservation, and statistical validity over standard isotropic models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: structured drift design improving geometry preservation in diffusion models, applicable to molecular and 3D generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出结构化漂移设计改进扩散模型，有助于分子和3D生成等应用
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出结构化漂移设计改进扩散模型，有助于分子和3D生成等应用
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出结构化漂移设计改进扩散模型，有助于分子和3D生成等应用
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces the Geometry-aware Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process, a structured drift design that embeds data geometry into diffusion dynamics, improving sample quality and avoiding mode averaging.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有扩散模型使用各向同性噪声破坏几何结构，导致收敛慢和模式平均。本文提出几何感知OU过程，将数据几何嵌入前向和反向扩散，保留多模态分布的结构。实验表明在生物遗传数据上生成更真实的样本，为后续扩散模型在科学数据上的应用提供了新范式。

quick_skim.title: HonestAffinity: Leak-Aware Evaluation of Protein and Pocket Priors for Binding Affinity Prediction
quick_skim.abstract: Sequence-based deep learning offers a scalable alternative to structure-based scoring for protein-ligand binding affinity prediction. However, progress is hard to interpret when architectural priors are evaluated on canonical PDBbind-style splits that leak similarity classes across folds. We present HonestAffinity, a compact 1D-input predictor to isolate two priors under a leak-aware protocol: frozen ESM-2 (650M) protein embeddings and a learned binary pocket-position marker. We evaluate a multi-scale convolutional/Transformer template in three variants: HonestAffinity-Pocket, HonestAffinity-NoPocket, and HonestAffinity-Pocket-NoESM. All three train on 11,513 LP-PDBBind complexes in ~3 GPU-hours. We benchmark against five baselines on the LP-PDBBind 3-tier no-leak hold-out, CASF-2016, and a CASF-2016 non-train subset. Our central finding is a split-conditioned reversal rather than a uniformly best prior: HonestAffinity-Pocket achieves the best mean Pearson R on validation and CASF-2016 splits, whereas HonestAffinity-Pocket-NoESM achieves the best mean Pearson R on every strict LP no-leak tier (test_cl1-cl3). Both the pocket marker and ESM-2 input improve performance on familiar splits but reduce Pearson R on strict no-leak tiers. We argue models should report paired canonical and leak-proof ablations, and that deployment-regime-matched variants better describe these reversals than a single default. Code and scripts are linked in the footnote; checkpoints will be released upon acceptance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: evaluates pocket priors for binding affinity, applicable to pocket-conditioned generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 评估口袋先验用于亲和力预测，可应用于口袋条件生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 评估口袋先验用于亲和力预测，可应用于口袋条件生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 评估口袋先验用于亲和力预测，可应用于口袋条件生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: HonestAffinity provides a leak-aware evaluation of protein and pocket priors for binding affinity prediction, offering reliable benchmarks for pocket-conditioned molecular generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 基于结构的配体生成需要可靠的口袋先验，但现有评估协议存在数据泄漏问题。本文提出HonestAffinity，一个紧凑的1D输入预测器，在无泄漏协议下隔离评估ESM-2蛋白质嵌入和口袋位置标记两种先验。在11313个复合物上训练仅需3GPU小时，并基准测试五种基线。结果揭示了口袋先验的重要性，为口袋条件分子生成方法提供了可靠评估基准。

quick_skim.title: An Agentic Platform for Drug Repurposing Unified across Molecular, Phenotypic, and Clinical Scales
quick_skim.abstract: Drug repurposing offers an effective path to new therapies, yet existing computational approaches rely on a single line of evidence and are rarely validated across biological scales. We present LinkD, an integrated framework that unifies diffusion-based affinity prediction, proteome-wide selectivity scoring, phenotypic validation, and population-scale clinical evidence. LinkD-Bind predicts binding across 14,981 drugs and 20,385 human targets, ranking first in 8 of 9 BindingDB, Davis, and KIBA evaluations, with the largest gains under cold-start conditions. LinkD-Select recovers 95.3% of known drug-target pairs by combining selectivity scoring and molecular docking. LinkD-Pheno integrates drug-sensitivity and CRISPR dependency data across 960 cancer cell lines, identifying 34 novel drug-gene pairs and recovering ~85% of known targets among the top 50 candidates. Across 11.5 million individuals from Mount Sinai and UK Biobank, LinkD-prioritized {beta}-blockers propranolol (HR 0.82) and carvedilol (HR 0.92) reduced 5-year prostate cancer incidence relative to metoprolol, corroborated by ADRB2 docking and LNCaP growth inhibition. LinkD-Agent, which can effectively orchestrate all evidence layers, is served on a publicly available web platform (https://linkd-agent.onrender.com/), enabling a wide range of users to derive new drug repurposing opportunities through natural language queries.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: diffusion-based affinity prediction for drug-target binding, part of pocket-ligand theme
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于扩散的亲和力预测用于药物-靶标结合，属于口袋-配体主题

quick_skim.title: Structure-guided compound prioritization strategy for virtual screening identifies putative binders for the nuclear receptor LRH-1
quick_skim.abstract: Compound ranking in structure-based virtual screening notoriously yields highly ranked false positive binders due to variable poses or biases in scoring terms. We developed a compound prioritization strategy that utilizes sampled docked poses from contrasting docking approaches (targeted physics-based docking and blind docking with a generative model) against multiple models of the target protein to train a multi-layer perceptron (MLP). The model predicts binders at the orthosteric ligand-binding pocket of the nuclear receptor LRH-1 (NR5A2). Our approach circumvents the reliance on a single docked pose for scoring compounds or individual scoring metrics for compound ranking. In a separate benchmarking set, we observed that the MLP identifies known binders that are chemically dissimilar from the compounds in the training set and is sensitive to single scaffold modifications, making it a potential tool for lead optimization. We applied our strategy to a prospective virtual screening campaign, which resulted in the discovery of four putative LRH-1 binders. We found that a combination of scoring and prediction metrics enriches for the hit compounds across library sizes. In all, this implementation presents a method to leverage structural and experimental data to aid virtual screening for a challenging protein target.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: structure-guided compound prioritization using generative model blind docking
